summary: Phase 1c spec review: FAIL across all five dimensions. The behavioral spec correctly captures the domain requirements from ui-fields.md, workflows.md, and aggregates.md at a high level, but contains four high-severity issues: (1) the tryNewTag import path bypasses the domain module's intended encapsulation boundary without acknowledgment, (2) REQ-TAG-007 has undefined behavior for blur-with-invalid-text, (3) activeFilterTags state persistence is incompatible with the existing snapshot-replacement pattern in feedReducer, and (4) the FeedDomainSnapshot.tagInventory extension is a cross-process concern not accounted for. The verification architecture is missing proof obligations for several documented edge cases (tag chip overflow EC-013, close-input-before-remove EC-018, blur-with-invalid-text error path). The edge case catalog is missing scenarios for simultaneous inputs, max tag length, note deletion during input, and makes an unsubstantiated claim about domain pipeline serialization in EC-012. Scope clarity is undermined by ambiguous sidebar placement wording. Total score 66/100 against threshold of 80.
